answer:
The range \(R_n\) is the number of distinct values visited by the random walk up to time \(n\). For a random walk with positive drift (since \(P(X_i=1)=0.8\)), the walk moves upward most of the time, so the number of distinct values is approximately the maximum minus the minimum, which grows linearly with \(n\). The expected range per step converges to the drift, which is \(0.8 \cdot 1 + 0.2 \cdot (-1) = 0.6\).

\boxed{I}
